strengths: Concept, requirements, build plan, and static artifact all preserve the same core: notice text becomes household action routes with source evidence.
The artifact avoids the recent AI meeting-room pattern and is legible to non-engineer family users.
Source snippets and uncertainty cards make the safety boundary visible instead of presenting AI output as official instruction.
The artifact validates with the existing artifact validator and includes metadata, diagrams, mockups, README, and self-review.

problems.issue: The demo uses fixed static extraction, so the input textarea does not produce new cards from arbitrary text.
problems.requiredChange: Keep this explicit as an MVP limitation; do not imply real AI parsing until a parser exists.

problems.issue: The first screen has dense Japanese text across three panels.
problems.requiredChange: During visual polish, check mobile and narrow desktop widths and shorten card copy if text feels crowded.

rewriteInstructions: Do not expand into PDF upload, email import, or school-system integration in the MVP.
If revising UI, preserve source evidence highlighting and the 'まだ判断しないこと' panel.
If adding real extraction later, treat dates, target students, deadlines, and safety-related notices as low-confidence until verified by the user.
